page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2g.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD storage volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps on a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the instance delivers modest processing capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the server in the bottom 25% tier. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks performs at an average level, as do Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference tasks. Conversely, compute-heavy operations like gzip compression and medium-model LLM inference show poor performance, falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.i2g.2xlarge offers a high density of local SSD storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial local caching or scratch space without the premium cost of high-tier compute instances. The trade-off lies in its older CPU architecture, which limits its suitability for CPU-intensive tasks. This instance is best suited for storage-centric workloads, database caching, lightweight web serving, and general-purpose applications that benefit from local SSD storage but do not demand high-performance CPU throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2g.2xlarge is an x86_64 server instance equipped with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs operating at 2.5 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest CPU capabilities, with single-core and multi-core processing scores falling in the bottom 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. Due to the older processor architecture, compute-heavy tasks like compression and medium-model LLM inference perform poorly. However, the substantial bundled local SSD storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for storage-centric applications. This instance is best suited for database caching, local storage-heavy workloads, and general-purpose tasks that require dedicated cores and local scratch space but do not demand high-performance compute capabilities.
